title: rourea coccinea subsp. boiviniana
intro: Rourea coccinea subsp. boiviniana is a plant species, also known as Madagascar's 'red heart', which belongs to the family Connaraceae. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Rourea coccinea subsp. boiviniana include the treatment of fever, wounds, and respiratory infections, as well as being used as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as infusions and decoctions, which are used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
